title: MAC operation not permitted lsetxattr(label=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0)
body:
### Issue Description

When running tests that use testcontainers and Podman I get:
`Status 500: {"cause":"operation not permitted","message":"lsetxattr(label=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0) /var/run/docker.sock: operation not permitted","response":500}
`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

The tests use Testcontainers (JVM) to run a Docker Compose file, and in this case, it fails with the above error. However, when I run the Docker Compose file separately from the command line, it works fine.
I'm unsure whether this issue should be reported under Podman or Testcontainers.


### Describe the results you received

Error:
`Status 500: {"cause":"operation not permitted","message":"lsetxattr(label=system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0) /var/run/docker.sock: operation not permitted","response":500}`

### Describe the results you expected

Docker compose runs normally

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I mean if they pass /var/run/docker.sock as source and also the relabel option `Z` then it will try to label that file which of course should not work when running rootless.
